title: SENTINEL APEX — Medium Security Vulnerability High EPSS CVE-2026-10732
id: 40588e86-fa20-5114-bfd6-3113c50ffe5a
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-10732 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-10732
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/05
modified: 2026/06/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-10732 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T08:30:15Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-10732", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-10732 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5514729; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-05, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attackers to Execute Arbitrary Code
items.description:
Microsoft has released a security update addressing a critical vulnerability in Microsoft Edge that could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on vulnerable systems. Tracked as CVE-2026-45495 and reported by Orange Tsai of DEVCORE, the flaw carries a CVSS v3 score of 7.5 and requires user interaction, for example, visiting a malicious webpage or […] 
 The post  Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attackers to Execute Arbitrary Code  appeared first on <a href="https://cybersec

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Burst Statistics Plugin
id: ef73515b-ae3b-5f8d-939e-4ea35e5f9569
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Burst Statistics Plugin (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Burst Statistics Plugin
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/05
modified: 2026/06/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Burst Statistics Plugin — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-05T08:30:15Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "Attackers Actively Exploiting Critical Vulnerability in Burst Statistics Plugin", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
